citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
At the time of inspection, one adult Patagonian cavy has an abnormal left front foot. This foot is swollen to at least twice
the size of the other foot and there are wounds on the front aspect of the swollen foot. The bottom portion of the left leg
also appears swollen. The back aspect of the foot has an area that appears wet with a reddish liquid and there is debris
sticking to this spot. When asked about the foot, the facility representative stated that this foot has been abnormal for a
while and that they were previously told by their attending veterinarian that the foot was enlarged due to scar tissue from
an old injury. The attending veterinarian told the inspector that they do not recall examining this animal. There is no
documentation from the facility or the attending veterinarian of previous examinations or what the foot looked like in
October 2025 when the attending veterinarian was last at the facility. During the inspection, the facility representative had
not noticed the wounds or wet areas that are present on the foot. Based on the current condition of the foot, there is an
active issue that needs veterinary attention.
Inadequate communication with the attending veterinarian and failure to observe medical problems can lead to the
prolonged suffering of animals when injuries or changes in their health or behavior are not noticed or communicated in a
timely manner. The facility must ensure that all animals are observed daily to assess their health and well-being and then
ensure a mechanism of direct and frequent communication with the attending veterinarian. The facility must obtain
veterinary care for this Patagonian cavy.
To be corrected by close of business on February 6, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
At the time of inspection, one adult Patagonian cavy has an abnormal left front foot. This foot is swollen to at least twice 
the size of the other foot and there are wounds on the front aspect of the swollen foot. The bottom portion of the left leg 
also appears swollen. The back aspect of the foot has an area that appears wet with a reddish liquid and there is debris 
sticking to this spot. When asked about the foot, the facility representative stated that this foot has been abnormal for a 
while and that they were previously told by their attending veterinarian that the foot was enlarged due to scar tissue from 
an old injury. The attending veterinarian told the inspector that they do not recall examining this animal. There is no 
documentation from the facility or the attending veterinarian of previous examinations or what the foot looked like in 
October 2025 when the attending veterinarian was last at the facility. During the inspection, the facility representative had 
not noticed the wounds or wet areas that are present on the foot. Based on the current condition of the foot, there is an 
active issue that needs veterinary attention.  
 
Inadequate communication with the attending veterinarian and failure to observe medical problems can lead to the 
prolonged suffering of animals when injuries or changes in their health or behavior are not noticed or communicated in a 
timely manner. The facility must ensure that all animals are observed daily to assess their health and well-being and then 
ensure a mechanism of direct and frequent communication with the attending veterinarian. The facility must obtain 
veterinary care for this Patagonian cavy. 
 
To be corrected by close of business on February 6, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
